input.name: Remove gh sr aw
input.overview: Delete the `gh sr aw` Cobra subtree (ports check/assign) from the CLI while keeping the underlying `ghawports` package for `gh sr doctor`. Update the agentic workflows guide to steer multi-instance same-host users to `runner_mode: container` and document the removal.
input.plan:
# Remove `gh sr aw` and document container mode

## Context

- The `aw` command is defined only in [`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go): `awCmd()` registers `gh sr aw ports {check,assign}`, which call [`internal/ghawports`](internal/ghawports) for MCP port lint and frontmatter port rewriting.
- **`internal/ghawports` and `internal/ghawfrontmatter` must stay.** [`internal/doctor/doctor.go`](internal/doctor/doctor.go) imports `ghawports` and runs the same checks via `--workflow-root` / `--repo` (see existing doc cross-reference in [§8a of `docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md)).
- No other code or tests reference `gh sr aw` (grep shows only `main.go` and the guide).

## Code changes

1. **[`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go)**
   - Remove `awCmd()` from `root.AddCommand(...)`.
   - Delete the entire `awCmd()` function (lines ~425–488).
   - Drop the unused import `"github.com/an-lee/gh-sr/internal/ghawports"`.

2. **Optional clarity in [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md)**  
   - Adjust the `internal/ghawports/` bullet so it no longer implies a dedicated CLI subcommand (e.g. note it backs `doctor` MCP port checks). Small, keeps contributor map accurate.

## Documentation changes

Primary file: [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md), section **8a** (concurrent jobs / MCP ports, native-only).

- Remove the two bullets that document `gh sr aw ports check` and `gh sr aw ports assign`.
- Replace with explicit guidance:
  - **Multiple concurrent agentic runner instances on the same physical host:** prefer [`runner_mode: container`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md#8b-runner-modes-native-vs-container-recommended-for-concurrency) (already described in §8b); that avoids host-level MCP port collisions and `/tmp/gh-aw` sharing without per-workflow port rewriting.
  - **If staying on native mode** with `count > 1`: still use per-instance `agentic_mcp_port_base` / `agentic_mcp_ports` and matching workflow `sandbox.mcp.port` + `gh-sr-mcp-*` labels as in the same section; use **`gh sr doctor --workflow-root …`** (or `--repo` when applicable) to validate frontmatter vs config — no separate `aw` command.
  - **One-line removal note:** `gh sr aw` was removed; users who previously used `ports assign` should either switch to container mode or maintain ports/labels manually (upstream [gh-aw sandbox docs](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) still apply).

No changes required to [`docs/content/commands.md`](docs/content/commands.md) or README (they do not list `aw` today).

## Verification (after implementation)

- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` from repo root.
- Smoke: `gh sr --help` no longer lists `aw`; `gh sr doctor --help` unchanged.

## Out of scope

- Deleting `internal/ghawports` or tests under it — would break `doctor`.
- Changing doctor behavior unless you later want to fold port checks elsewhere.

todos.content: Remove awCmd(), root registration, and ghawports import from cmd/gh-sr/main.go
Revise docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md §8a: drop aw bullets; document container mode for concurrency; doctor + native caveats
Optional: clarify ghawports role in CLAUDE.md (doctor, not aw CLI)
Run go build ./... and go test ./...

input.old_string:
The gh-aw MCP gateway listens on the host network (`docker --network host`). The TCP port comes from workflow frontmatter `sandbox.mcp.port` (default **80** after `gh aw compile`). If several agentic jobs run on the **same physical host**, they must use **different** ports, or only one such job may run at a time.

**Configure per-instance ports in gh-sr** (`runners.yml`): on a runner with `profile: agentic`, set either:
input.new_string:
The gh-aw MCP gateway listens on the host network (`docker --network host`). The TCP port comes from workflow frontmatter `sandbox.mcp.port` (default **80** after `gh aw compile`). If several agentic jobs run on the **same physical host**, they must use **different** ports, or only one such job may run at a time.

For **multiple concurrent agentic runner instances on one host**, prefer [`runner_mode: container`](#8b-runner-modes-native-vs-container-recommended-for-concurrency) instead of juggling ports and labels in native mode: each runner container gets its own network namespace and MCP gateway on port 80 without conflicts (see §8b).

**Configure per-instance ports in gh-sr** (`runners.yml`): on a runner with `profile: agentic`, set either:

input.old_string:
**CLI helpers** (run from a clone of the repository that contains `.github/workflows/*.md`):

- `gh sr aw ports check [--workflow-root DIR] [--repo owner/repo]` — warns on duplicate ports, implicit default 80 across files, and rough concurrency vs. `count` on each host.
- `gh sr aw ports assign [--workflow-root DIR] [--base-port 9080] [--write]` — writes distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values per workflow markdown (dry-run unless `--write`). It does **not** modify `features.mcp-gateway`; that flag is separate (see [gh-aw Sandbox configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) for gateway routing and `sandbox.mcp`). Re-run `gh aw compile` afterward.
- `gh sr doctor --workflow-root DIR` (or `doctor --repo owner/repo` when `./.github/workflows` exists in the current directory) — runs the same MCP port checks after host diagnostics.
input.new_string:
**Validation** (run from a clone of the repository that contains `.github/workflows/*.md`):

- `gh sr doctor --workflow-root DIR` (or `doctor --repo owner/repo` when `./.github/workflows` exists in the current directory) — runs MCP port checks (duplicate ports, implicit default 80, rough concurrency vs. `count` on each host) after host diagnostics.

The former `gh sr aw ports check` / `gh sr aw ports assign` commands were removed: use **`runner_mode: container`** for same-host concurrency without rewriting workflow frontmatter. If you stay on native mode with `count > 1`, keep ports and `gh-sr-mcp-*` labels aligned manually and re-run `gh aw compile` after editing frontmatter; see [gh-aw Sandbox configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) for `sandbox.mcp` and `features.mcp-gateway`.
- `internal/ghawports/` — MCP port lint for agentic workflow markdown (used by `gh sr doctor`, not a separate CLI)

Here is what was done:

### Code
- **[`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go)** — Removed the `ghawports` import, dropped `awCmd()` from `root.AddCommand`, and deleted the whole `awCmd()` implementation (`aw` / `ports` / `check` / `assign`).

### Docs
- **[`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md)** (§8a) — Added a short note to prefer **`runner_mode: container`** for several concurrent agentic instances on one host. Replaced the old “CLI helpers” list with a **Validation** bullet for `gh sr doctor`, plus a paragraph explaining removal of `gh sr aw ports check|assign`, container mode as the replacement for bulk port assignment, and manual frontmatter + `gh aw compile` if you stay on native with `count > 1`.

### Contributor note
- **[`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md)** — Clarified that `internal/ghawports` is used by **`gh sr doctor`**, not a separate CLI.

`internal/ghawports` and `internal/ghawfrontmatter` are unchanged; `doctor` still uses them.

### Verification
- **`go build ./...`** — OK  
- **`go test ./...`** — OK  

[VERDICT] Removed `gh sr aw` from `cmd/gh-sr/main.go`. Updated §8a in `docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md` and the `ghawports` line in `CLAUDE.md`. Build: OK. Tests: OK. Watch: anything (scripts, external docs) that still invoked `gh sr aw ports …` needs to use `gh sr doctor` for checks and container mode or manual ports for multi-instance native.

For same-host concurrency without hand-maintaining distinct `sandbox.mcp.port` values per workflow, use **`runner_mode: container`** (see §8b). If you stay on native mode with `count > 1`, align ports, `gh-sr-mcp-*` labels, and `runs-on` yourself and re-run `gh aw compile` after editing frontmatter; see [gh-aw Sandbox configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/sandbox/) for `sandbox.mcp` and `features.mcp-gateway`.

content.text:
Here is what changed:

### Removed packages
- Deleted [`internal/ghawports/`](internal/ghawports/) (`check.go`, `assign.go`, `check_test.go`).
- Deleted [`internal/ghawfrontmatter/`](internal/ghawfrontmatter/) (`ghawfrontmatter.go`, `ghawfrontmatter_test.go`) — it was only used by `ghawports`.

### `gh sr doctor`
- [`internal/doctor/doctor.go`](internal/doctor/doctor.go): Dropped `ghawports` import and the whole **Agentic MCP ports (workflow markdown)** path (`runAgenticMCPWorkflowCheck`, `resolveWorkflowRoot`).
- `Run` signature is now  
  `Run(w, cfgPath, envPath, cfg, cfgErr, gh, filterHost, filterRepo, strict)` — no `workflowRoot`.
- [`cmd/gh-sr/main.go`](cmd/gh-sr/main.go): Removed `--workflow-root` and the extra `doctor.Run` argument.
- [`internal/tui/dashboard.go`](internal/tui/dashboard.go): Updated `doctor.Run` call.
- [`internal/doctor/doctor_test.go`](internal/doctor/doctor_test.go): Updated `Run` calls to match.

### Docs / project notes
- [`docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md`](docs/content/guides/agentic-workflows.md) §8a: Replaced the old doctor/MCP-lint bullets with **Operational checks** text that states doctor no longer scans workflow markdown for MCP ports and points to container mode / manual alignment.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md): Dropped `ghawfrontmatter` / `ghawports` from the architecture list and removed the `ghawports` key-files line.

### Verification
`go build ./...`, `go test ./...`, and `go vet ./...` all succeed.

[VERDICT] Removed `internal/ghawports` and `internal/ghawfrontmatter`; `doctor` no longer runs MCP port lint or accepts `--workflow-root`. Build/tests/vet: OK. Watch: any scripts or docs outside this repo that used `gh sr doctor --workflow-root` should drop that flag; native multi-instance setups need manual port/label alignment or `runner_mode: container`.

[REDACTED]
